Get Started
System Understanding Infrastructure · AI-Native Operations

Operational Memory
for AI Agents.

AI made writing code free. The bottleneck is now what happens after it ships — connection pools nobody documented, compliance windows your on-call forgot, cascading failures at 3am. Mergen is the operational memory and safety layer: it compresses raw production telemetry into structured machine context and encodes your team's override decisions as enforceable policy that compounds with every incident you resolve.

Get Started →Star
npx mergen-server@latest setup
✓ Override Corpus✓ Agent Blunder Log✓ No Datadog Required✓ MCP-native stdio✓ Local-first · VPC-ready✓ ≥85% confidence gate
94%Root cause accuracy33-incident eval corpus
31/33Correct classifications2 known false positives
10/10Infra failure classesdetected at 100%
≥85%Confidence gatebefore any autonomous action
mergen — autonomous incident loop

Watch: 60s Quick Start

01 // The Paradigm Shift

Stop jumping
between dashboards.

The Manual Loop

0m
PagerDuty fires
Engineer wakes up, opens laptop.
5m
Log Grepping
Searching through millions of lines in ELK/Datadog.
15m
Dashboard Jumping
Correlating metrics across 5 different tabs.
30m
Slack War Room
Asking "who deployed last?" and "is the DB down?"
45m
Manual Fix
SSHing into boxes, manual rollbacks, praying it works.
60m+
Validation
Watching dashboards for another 15m to be sure.

The Mergen Loop

0m
PagerDuty → Mergen
Autonomous loop triggered immediately.
2s
Causal Analysis
800+ events analyzed across all telemetry.
5s
Corpus Check
Past overrides and policies consulted.
10s
Autonomous Fix
Fix executed with ≥85% confidence.
1m
Validated
Error rate confirmed at 0. Incident resolved.
2m
Audit Trail
Full postmortem posted to Slack for review.

Result: 96% reduction in MTTR. The engineer wakes up to a resolved incident and a full audit trail, rather than a 3am fire drill.

02 // Accuracy

Evaluated before
you ever use it.

We built a regression eval harness before shipping v1. Every PR that touches detector logic must pass this suite — 33 real incident scenarios, 10 infrastructure failure classes. The suite fails CI if a previously-detected scenario stops being classified correctly.

Overall accuracy
94%
31 of 33 incidents classified correctly
Corpus size33 incidents
Correct31 / 33
False positives2 (probe errors)
Last eval runJun 16, 2026
Failure classFixturesAccuracy
DB Connection Pool
5/5
100%
OOM Kill
5/5
100%
Rate Limit Cascade
3/3
100%
Certificate Expiry
3/3
100%
Disk Pressure
3/3
100%
Service Unavailable
3/3
100%
Slow Query
2/2
100%
Downstream Latency
3/3
100%
Queue Backlog
3/3
100%
Upstream Error
1/1
100%
False positive (probe/scrape errors)
Fires on /health and /metrics — human override needed
0/2
0%

Why this matters: Most observability tools are evaluated by the engineers who built them, on the incidents they chose. Mergen ships a public eval harness — the same suite that gates every release. When we say 94% root cause accuracy, that number is reproducible and falsifiable. The 2 failures are documented: the detector fires on liveness probe and Prometheus scrape errors when it shouldn't.

View Full JSON Baseline →
02 // Why Now

Everyone is building
the accelerator pedal.

Every other startup is making AI write code faster. Three structural flaws of AI code generation are compounding in parallel — each one making the layer below the IDE more necessary.

01

Velocity Trap

Spaghetti automation at scale

AI agents produce syntactically perfect code that ignores systemic architecture constraints. The result is high-velocity technical debt shipped at machine speed. When code generation is free, production safety becomes the scarcest resource in the org.

02

Destroyed Memory

No one knows why it was built that way

When a human spent three days on a complex routing loop, context lived in their head. When an AI generates the same block in four seconds, nobody remembers why it exists. The moment it hits production, it becomes instant, unmaintainable legacy.

03

Incident Surge

Failures that only appear under load

Autonomous agents shipping code at machine speed introduce distributed failures that escape all static tests — connection pool exhaustion, timeout cascades, silent microservice regressions. These only manifest in production, at 3am.

Mergen is the brakes, the steering wheel, and the black-box flight recorder. It scales directly in proportion to the failures of AI-generated code — and the override corpus compounds with every incident your team resolves. Six months in, your operational DNA is impossible to replicate from a standing start.

Talk to us
03 // Capabilities

The moat is what
accumulates.

01

Autonomous Incident Loop

PagerDuty fires → causal analysis across all telemetry → override corpus consulted → fix executes at ≥85% confidence → validated (error count before/after) → RESOLVED posted to your Slack thread.

MRG
Mergen APP 3:17 PM
✅ Incident #402 resolved autonomously
Audit Trail Summary
• Root Cause: DB Connection Pool exhaustion (api-service)
• Confidence: 91% (matches pattern: stuck_idle_connections)
• Action: Flushed idle pools & increased capacity (max_idle: 5 → 20)
• Validation: Error rate 14% → 0.02% (confirmed 3:18 PM)

02

Override Corpus

Every time your team overrides Mergen's recommendation, that decision is encoded as policy. After six months of production: your Friday settlement windows, your compliance holds, your on-call's preferred fixes — structured, queryable, and impossible to replicate from a standing start.

03

Agent Blunder Log

Every autonomous action Mergen's safety layer blocks is recorded with its reason: allowlist block, RBAC rejection, override corpus halt, planning gate denial. The headline number is "prevented" — autonomous actions your on-call didn't have to handle.

04

Institutional Memory

Every resolved incident generates a structured postmortem, indexed by BM25 + TF-IDF hybrid retrieval. When an agent opens a file, the IDE receives relevant past incidents automatically.

auth_middleware.ts — Mergen Contextmcp.json
// Mergen: Historical Context found
⚠️ This file was modified in Incident #388 (OOM Kill).
Reason: Recursive token validation on nested JWTs.
Constraint: Do not increase stack depth > 4.
export const validateToken = (token: string) => {
  // checking depth...

05

Measurable MTTR

Designed for the sleep-deprived on-call engineer. Mergen closes the loop without manual dashboard jumping or log grepping. The impact report tracks autonomous MTTR vs. manual MTTR per failure mode.

06

Local-First. VPC-Ready.

Mergen runs entirely on your infrastructure. Point it at Docker containers or drop one import into your Node.js entry point. PII shield is always on. Execution audit trail written to ~/.mergen/audit.log as immutable JSONL.

04 // Interactive Sandbox

Test the detector
logic.

Select Scenario

// Telemetry Input: pg_stat_activity count=98/100, wait_event=ClientRead
Select a scenario and click "Run Detector" to see Mergen's causal logic in action.

Route your context.

→

Layer

Windsurfanalyze_production
VS Codemcp.json
05 // Getting Started

Up in five minutes.
Production-ready in a week.

01
npm / binary

Install

One command sets up Mergen, walks you through integrations, and creates the config file.

npx mergen-server@latest setup

Requires Node.js 18+. Binaries available for macOS / Linux / Windows.

02
PagerDuty · OTLP · Docker

Connect Your Stack

Point your PagerDuty webhook at Mergen, set your OTLP exporter endpoint, or stream Docker logs in one curl.

# PagerDuty → Service → Webhooks → https://your-host:3000/webhooks/pagerduty
# OTLP (any language)
OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT=http://127.0.0.1:3000 node app.js
# Docker logs
curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:3000/watchers/docker

No Datadog required. Start with Docker logs — it works from day one.

03
Claude Code · Cursor · VS Code

Add to Your AI IDE

Register Mergen as an MCP server. The tools — triage_incident, analyze_runtime, validate_fix — appear automatically in your IDE.

# Claude Code
claude mcp add mergen --transport stdio -- node "$(pwd)/server/dist/index.js"

# Cursor / Windsurf / VS Code
# .cursor/mcp.json and .vscode/mcp.json are already committed in this repo

Ask: "What caused the last incident?" — Mergen answers with root cause + fix hint.

04
optional · ≥85% confidence gate

Enable Autopilot

Set MERGEN_AUTOPILOT=true to let Mergen execute fixes autonomously. Starts in shadow mode for 30 days — builds a track record before it acts.

MERGEN_AUTOPILOT=true mergen-server start
# Shadow mode (observe only, no execution):
# omit MERGEN_AUTOPILOT — triage_incident still available on demand

Every blocked action is recorded in the Agent Blunder Log. Audit trail at ~/.mergen/audit.log.

Full Install Guide →Talk to us about your stack
